name: Adult-onset GHR disruption improved insulin sensitivity and extended female mouse lifespan
summary: Adult GHR disruption is an important bridge from developmental dwarf-mouse evidence toward adult-onset GH/IGF attenuation.
statement: Disrupting growth hormone receptor in mature-adult mice induced GH resistance and reduced IGF-1, improved male insulin sensitivity and neoplasm profile, and extended median and maximal lifespan in females.
population_or_model: Mature-adult mice with adult-onset growth hormone receptor gene disruption
quantitative_note: The intervention was initiated in mature adulthood rather than through lifelong germline deficiency.
caveats: This remains mouse genetic evidence, not a human intervention.
Effects differed by sex and endpoint.
Gene disruption is not equivalent to a clinically deployable partial endocrine modulation strategy.
